description:
You are acting as a penetration tester who needs a quick-and-dirty local “vulnerability-scanning” workspace before running any real scans.  
    Your goal (and the only step the automated grader will check) is to create a very small, one-file configuration that other tools will later consume.

    Required end-state of the system
    --------------------------------
    1. Directory: /home/user/vuln_scans  
       • Must exist.  
    2. File: /home/user/vuln_scans/default_scan.conf  
       • Must exist and be **exactly one** text line long.  
       • The line must read (without quotes and with a single trailing newline):
         SCAN_TARGET=127.0.0.1

    Constraints
    -----------
    • Achieve the whole setup with a **single terminal command**.  
    • You do **not** need to run any actual scanner; simply create the directory and file with the contents above.  
    • Do not include additional spaces, blank lines or comments in default_scan.conf.  
    • The agent will not have root privileges; stay within /home/user.  

    What the automated tests will verify
    ------------------------------------
    1. That the directory /home/user/vuln_scans exists.  
    2. That the file /home/user/vuln_scans/default_scan.conf exists.  
    3. That the file’s content is byte-for-byte:  
       “SCAN_TARGET=127.0.0.1\n” (where “\n” is a single newline).  

    You only need to run one command to satisfy all three checks.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth data for the autograder -->
    <dir path="/home/user/vuln_scans" must_exist="yes"/>
    <file path="/home/user/vuln_scans/default_scan.conf" must_exist="yes">
SCAN_TARGET=127.0.0.1
    </file>
